summary: Unify role + requirement into one `Requirement`; retire `Role`/`[role.*]`; roster and coverage both become checks over the one requirement

per.section: Decision: role and requirement are one concept

new.description: The renamed roster test suite (was tests/role_roster.rs), rewritten over `[requirement.<name>]` tables: the set-scope predicates (match selection, count, membership, unique, typed-ref/conforms-to, verified_by) and admissibility now fire over the unified `Requirement`. No `role` term in the file (naming rule, `specs/90-spec-system.md`).

edit.description: Fold every `[role.*]` facet (artifact→`kind`, contract, `match` selector, count/membership/unique, degree, verified_by) AND the requirement facets (means — now OPTIONAL per `specs/10-contracts.md` "all facets optional except its name" — satisfies fill, required) onto ONE `Requirement` struct keyed by name. Parse `[requirement.<name>]` as the unified table; retire the `Role` struct, the `[role.<name>]` root parse, and every `Role*` ComposeError variant (hard-cut — the surface is unpublished, a clean cut is preferred per inbox). Expand the requirement key allowlist (UNKNOWN-KEY-REJECT) from `means`/`required` to the unified facet set; the inbox scopes stray-key rejection to `[requirement.*]`+`[kind.*]`+predicate clauses, NO `[role.*]`. Keep `MatchSelector`/`CountBound`/`Membership`/`DegreeBound`/`EdgeBound`; rename `RoleContract`→ the requirement's contract type. Preserve the mutually-exclusive required⟷count parse rule on the unified type.

Re-cast `check` (selection), `conformance` (conforms-to), and `admissibility` plus the set-scope predicates (match/count/membership/unique/typed-ref/verified_by) as checks over `Requirement` instead of `Role`. Multiplicity is the facet over the requirement's filler set; the module doc drops the `role` framing for `requirement`.

Keep the referential presence check (unfilled + dangling `satisfies`) over the now-unified `Requirement`. `means` is optional, so a requirement with no `means` still gates coverage via `required`; the module stays separate from roster (kinship noted, merge still rejected).

`degree` reads its `DegreeBound` off `Requirement` instead of `Role`; a matched node's `(kind, id)` uses the requirement's `kind` facet (was `Role::artifact`). Update the module doc's per-role framing to per-requirement.

Thread `layer.requirements()` into the `roster::{admissibility,check,conformance}` and `graph::degree` call sites (today `layer.roles()` at ~436/440/448/481); `coverage::check` already reads `layer.requirements()` (~497). Remove the now-dead `layer.roles()` accessor usage.

Re-record the parse snapshots for the unified surface: `[requirement.*]` carries the folded facets; `[role.*]` no longer parses (assert it is now an unknown-root / rejected). Update insta snapshots deliberately.
Update for the unified `Requirement`: a requirement with no `means` still gates coverage; the existing unfilled/dangling assertions hold over the unified type.
Degree fixtures declare their bound under `[requirement.*]` instead of `[role.*]`; re-record any affected snapshot.

retire: src/compose.rs `[role.<name>]` parse + all Role* ComposeError variants

tests.asserts: the set-scope predicates (match/count/membership/unique/typed-ref/conforms/verified_by) + admissibility fire over `[requirement.*]`; a `[role.*]` table is now rejected.
unfilled/dangling coverage runs over the unified requirement; a means-less requirement still gates.

acceptance: `role` and `requirement` are one `Requirement` in code (no `Role`, no `[role.*]`, no `filled_by`); the roster set-scope predicates AND coverage both run over the one requirement; `means` is optional; unknown-key reject covers the unified `[requirement.*]` facet set; the naming rule (`specs/90-spec-system.md`) holds — no `role` term in code; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green with snapshots re-recorded deliberately.
notes: Inbox MAJOR RECONCILE + CONSOLIDATION (spec 0784d00). One atomic entry: the type unification ripples through compose/roster/coverage/graph/main at once, so it can't be split green-at-each-step — sole `open` entry, no parallel wave, no shared-file conflict. Folds the inbox UNKNOWN-KEY scope note. Hard-cut `[role.*]` (surface unpublished). COVERAGE-HARDEN + UNKNOWN-KEY-REJECT already shipped (6969683).

summary: Custom-kind units join coverage — read `[representation]` `satisfies` in `kind::Unit` instead of hardcoding it empty

gate.reason: follow-on; no story yet demands custom-kind coverage participation, and it must rebase onto the unified Requirement after CONSOLIDATE-REQUIREMENT lands

edit.description: Populate `Features.satisfies` for a custom-kind `Unit` from its projected `[representation]` rather than the hardcoded `satisfies: Vec::new()` (the commented gap in `kind::Unit`), so a custom kind (temper's own `spec` corpus) can opt into requirements — coverage is kind-blind by design. Confirm `import`'s custom-unit projection emits `[representation]` for the read to find.
A custom-kind unit declaring `satisfies` covers a requirement, exactly as a skill/rule does.

tests.asserts: a custom-kind unit's `satisfies` participates in coverage (kind-blind).

acceptance: A custom-kind unit's authored `satisfies` participates in coverage exactly as a skill/rule does; temper's own `spec` corpus can opt into requirements; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Inbox DEFER (1). kind::Unit hardcodes `satisfies=Vec::new()` today (commented gap). Coverage is kind-blind (spec), so the seam is legitimate — deferred only on priority. Shares coverage.rs with CONSOLIDATE-REQUIREMENT, so it must land AFTER that (rebase onto the unified `Requirement`); deferred anyway, never open during the consolidation. Revive when the dogfood needs the spec corpus in coverage.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: npm entry with platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's distribution pattern); a small launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`).
Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infrastructure, mostly outside `src/` — the flume cargo gates (fmt/clippy/test) can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human-held creds (npm org, tap repo, signing). Parked until a human sets those up; the mechanical wrapper/launcher is buildable once they exist. `(binary-bootstrap)` is RESOLVED — this is execution, not a fork.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md`, typed frontmatter + byte-faithful body, with its own decidable contract

gate.reason: reframe deprioritizes more built-in kinds; revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The Agent IR — the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs` end-to-end. Split YAML frontmatter into typed fields (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`) with every unknown key preserved verbatim in `extra`, a byte-faithful markdown body, and `provenance { source_path, import_hash }`. Ship `from_source_file`, `to_meta_document`, and `from_surface_dir`. Body copied byte-for-byte; only the header rewritten. Identity is the path (file stem), not an internal field (`specs/10-contracts.md`).

The built-in decidable floor for the `agent` kind, embedded like `contracts/rule.toml`. Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]` (harness fact); `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores (`globs`/`alwaysApply`); an advisory `max_lines`. No heuristic clause (`specs/00-intent.md` law 3).
Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an insta import round-trip proving idempotence.

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this entry owns the `pub mod` line).
Extend `Workspace` with `agents: Vec<Agent>` loaded under `<dir>/agents/*`, and add an `Agent(#[from] AgentError)` arm to `WorkspaceError`. Update the load tests.
Add `pub fn agent_features(&Agent) -> Features` mirroring `rule_features`: typed fields as scalars, every `extra` key folded into the name-keyed map, `body_lines` over the byte-faithful body.

Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to `<into>/agents/<name>/{meta.toml, AGENT.md}`, and add a `[[agent]]` lock row per agent — parallel to the rule path. Extend the idempotence snapshot to cover an agents fixture.

Wire the `agent` kind into `gate()`: embed `BUILTIN_AGENT_CONTRACT`, extract `agent_features` over `ws.agents`, compose the effective contract (floor ⊕ layer), extend the admissibility + conformance streams, add `"agent"` to `by_kind`, and add the agent floor to `schema`'s `floors`.

tests.asserts: Each decidable clause of contracts/agent.toml fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the embedded agent contract (conformance) and the agent contract passes admissibility; `temper import` scans, projects, and round-trips agents idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; `temper schema --kind agent` emits the agent contract; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred by the requirements/satisfies reframe: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction; value is meaningful contracts + the representation surface. Revive only if a story demands the `agent` kind — files/acceptance stay valid; mirrors the rule kind end-to-end. On revival re-check its shared-surface blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) serializes against any then-open entry.
